events.title: Highest temperature in Hong Kong on June 10?
events.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded by the Hong Kong Observatory in degrees Celsius on 10 Jun '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the Hong Kong Observatory, specifically the "Absolute Daily Max (deg. C)" the specified date once information is finalized in the relevant "Daily Extract", available here: https://www.weather.gov.hk/en/cis/climat.htm

This market can not resolve until data for this date has been published.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures in Celsius to one decimal place (eg, 9.1°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is initially published for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Current forecasts from Hong Kong Observatory models and regional consensus place the daytime high near 28°C on June 10, aligning with the seasonal average high of 31°C and an above-normal temperature outlook for June–August 2026 driven by long-term warming trends. Persistent humidity above 80% and limited rainfall expected that day support modest surface heating without strong convective cooling, reinforcing trader consensus around this threshold. Uncertainties remain from potential forecast revisions, localized cloud buildup, or shifts in steering patterns that could lower the peak by 1–2°C, while stronger insolation or reduced moisture would be required to push readings to 29°C or higher.

events.title: Highest temperature in Seoul on June 10?
events.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Incheon Intl Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 10 Jun '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day for the Incheon Intl Airport Station, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/kr/incheon/RKSI.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve until the first data point for the following date has been published on the resolution source.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Revisions to temperatures recorded within this market's timeframe will be considered until the first datapoint for the following date has been published, after which any alterations will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Official forecasts from the Korea Meteorological Administration indicate a daily maximum near 23°C for Seoul on June 10, supported by current observations of mostly cloudy skies, light northwest winds, and temperatures holding in the mid-20s Celsius range with minimal warming expected through peak afternoon hours. This aligns with typical early-June climatology for the region, where marine influences and cloud cover often cap highs below seasonal averages around 25–27°C. Model consensus shows limited potential for additional heating absent clear skies or southerly flow shifts. The 100% market-implied probability on 23°C reflects strong trader confidence in these stable conditions, though an unexpected clearing trend or measurement variance at official stations could still alter the recorded peak before resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: National Weather Service observations at LaGuardia Airport, the official Polymarket resolution station for New York City, recorded a daily maximum of 80–81°F on June 9 under partly sunny skies and light southwesterly flow. This aligns precisely with the market’s 100% implied probability for the 80-81°F outcome and sits near the early-June climatological average high of roughly 79°F. Model consensus from NOAA and private forecasters had converged on this narrow range in the final 24–48 hours, with minimal spread across guidance. A realistic challenge would require a post-analysis revision to the official reading or a station-specific discrepancy exceeding the two-degree band, both of which are rare once preliminary data are verified.
